reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures the informal problem despite two apparent differences that are in fact equivalent given the geometry. (1) `line[ℝ, P, Q]` is the full affine line, not just the chord segment — but since P, Q lie on the circle of radius 9 and the disk of radius 1 centered at (r,0) with r ≤ 8 is contained in the disk of radius 9, the extension of the line beyond the segment PQ has |x| > 9 and thus cannot meet the small disk; so line ∩ disk = chord ∩ disk here. (2) `Metric.sphere` is the boundary circle, not the closed disk — but since P and Q are outside the small disk (as |P|=|Q|=9 > r+1 for r<8, with only measure-zero boundary cases at r=8), the line meets the disk iff it meets the boundary circle. The parametrization via (9cos(2πs), 9sin(2πs)) with s uniform on [0,1] yields the uniform distribution on Ω, and the Lebesgue volume of the intersecting (s,t)-set equals the probability. The minimizer set `{r ∈ Icc 0 8 | ProbIntersect r = ⨅ x ∈ Icc 0 8, ProbIntersect x}` is the correct "values of r minimizing the probability".
